transforms: Downloaded HDX package metadata and XLSX resource.
Parsed the admin1, admin2, admin3, and Location sheets from the XLSX resource.
Added HDX/OCHA admin1 P-codes to matching governorate records.

Added HDX/OCHA admin2 P-codes to matching district records. The direct HDX/OCHA administrative-boundary alignment for Qadmous is documented in imports/manifests/hdx-syr-admin-boundaries-2026-06-28.json.
Added HDX/OCHA admin3 P-codes to matching subdistrict records.
Filled missing subdistrict Arabic names from HDX/OCHA admin3 names where the value was previously absent.
Imported only Location rows where location_type is Community.
Deferred Location rows where location_type is Neighbourhood because the current schema does not intentionally model neighbourhood-level records.
Mapped HDX admin P-codes to existing OpenSyria governorate, district, and subdistrict IDs using source names, aliases, and a documented crosswalk for known naming or vintage differences.
Used HDX coordinates as seed representative centroids.
Used HDX Location_Pcode values as OCHA P-code external identifiers.
Compared locality coordinates against current ADM3 polygons as QA while keeping parent relationships based on HDX OCHA admin P-codes.
reviewNotes: Seeded 7,593 locality records from HDX Community rows and deferred 414 Neighbourhood rows. Added OCHA P-codes to 14 governorates, 61 district records during the locality import, and 272 subdistricts. Qadmous was later aligned as the 62nd current district through the direct HDX/OCHA administrative-boundary manifest. Filled 6 missing subdistrict Arabic names from HDX admin3 values. Geometry QA found 7,555 records where the coordinate matched the mapped current ADM3 polygon, 37 records outside current ADM3 polygons, and 1 record where geometry and HDX P-code mapping differed. Edge cases are retained with record-level notes because the parent relationship follows the source's explicit OCHA admin P-code.
